**After the hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
The "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018 is adopted and incorporated herein by reference.
Requests for Production of Documents
Documents must be in the possession, custody or control of defendant in order to be discoverable from it. I.e., defendant is not required to produce documents maintained by affiliates. Defendant is not required to produce privileged documents.
1, 3, 5, 26, 44: MOOT. Defendant has agreed to produce responsive documents after entry of a protective order.
6, 14-22, 28-35, 38-40, 42-43, 50, 52-55, 65-73: GRANTED IN PART. Defendant is to produce documents as set out in the "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018.
Requests for Admission
1-6, 8: DENIED. Defendant's responses are adequate.
7, 9-12, 15-17: GRANTED IN PART, as outlined in the "report and recommendation" dated September 20, 2018.
Interrogatories
15.1, 17.1: MOOT. Defendant has agreed to supplement its responses.
50.5, 50.6: DENIED.
Sanctions
No sanctions are awarded to either party.
Counsel for Broadband is required to resubmit a proposed order which repeats VERBATIM the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.**

PLAINTIFF BROADBAND ITV, INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted: Plaintiff Broadband ITV, Inc.'s motion for protective order is granted in part. The parties are required to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a protective order that: 1) provides for two levels of confidential information called "Highly Confidential" and "Confidential"; 2) either side may designate any information as either Highly Confidential or Confidential subject to a motion by the other side challenging that designation; 3) both Highly Confidential and Confidential Information may be used by the receiving party only for this case and must be returned at the conclusion of the case with a declaration from an attorney stating that all such information has been returned; 4) all information designated Highly Confidential may only be provided to the attorneys, one outside expert and one person affiliated with the receiving party, which can include Mr. Diaz in the case of Broadband; 5) defendant OpenTV, Inc. is not required to provide any source code to Broadband; 6) all information designated Confidential may only be provided to persons who are working on this case for the receiving party; 7) prior to receiving any information designated Highly Confidential the person receiving such information must identify in a declaration filed under seal in this case with the specificity required for a CCP 2019.210 disclosure all non-public information known to that person that in any way relates to the subject matter of the Highly Confidential information; and 8) no person receiving any information designated Highly Confidential or Confidential is subject to any patent prosecution bar. A further hearing will be held on September 26, 2018 in the event that the parties are not able to agree on the language of a protective order in accordance with this ruling before that date. OpenTV's cross-motion for a protective order is moot and taken off calendar. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/HEK)
